Let  
                         τ 
                       be the set of all “tasks” that was introduced in premise ( 
                         
                            
                               P 
                            
                            
                               2 
                            
                         
                      ). In the real-world setting, curricula  
                         C 
                       are constructed such that  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                         
                       is a proper subset of  
                         τ 
                       consisting of tasks that span selected learning objectives. By using toy models of the real-world pair  
                         
                            ( 
                            μ 
                            , 
                            τ 
                            ) 
                         
                       of Section  2 , the arguments in Section  3  suggest that  
                         C 
                       must be selected judiciously if the order in which tasks should be performed is to be well-defined (cf. the last sentence of  Example 3.3 ). Such well-defined orders are vital to the development of “prerequisite-structures” on the courses of a curriculum, which is at the forefront of the following sections. Thus, for the remainder of this paper, every curriculum  
                         C 
                       is assumed adequate so that the relation  
                         
                            
                               ≲ 
                            
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                         
                       is a partial order on  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                         
                      
 ( Theorem 3.4 (1)).

The reader whose interests are solely mathematical should note that the arguments of this section are valid if  
                         
                            ( 
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≲ 
                               
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                                  C 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       is replaced by any partially ordered set  
                         
                            ( 
                            P 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                      , with an “adequate curriculum  
                         C 
                      ” taken to mean any nonempty finite collection of nonempty subsets of  
                         P 
                       such that  
                         
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                            = 
                            P 
                         
                      . In fact,  Theorem 3.6  shows that every such collection can be realized as an adequate curriculum  
                         C 
                       with  
                         
                            
                               ( 
                               P 
                               , 
                               ≤ 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            
                               ( 
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     ≲ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     C 
                                  
                               
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      . Therefore, the work in this section can be regarded as a pure investigation into the structure of partially ordered sets.

If the applicability to academic programs is of interest, then the reader may choose to consider only those curricula  
                         C 
                       that contain an  admissions course  (see  Remark 2.7 (2)), which we define as any  
                         
                            A 
                            ∈ 
                            C 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            x 
                            ∈ 
                            A 
                         
                       for every  
                         
                            x 
                            ∈ 
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            x 
                            
                               
                                  ≲ 
                               
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                                  C 
                               
                            
                            y 
                         
                       for some  
                         
                            y 
                            ∈ 
                            A 
                         
                       (i.e.,  
                         A 
                       is a semi-ideal of the partially ordered set  
                         
                            ( 
                            ⋃ 
                            C 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≲ 
                               
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                                  C 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      ), and  
                         
                            c 
                            ∩ 
                            A 
                            = 
                            ∅ 
                         
                       for every  
                         
                            c 
                            ∈ 
                            C 
                            ∖ 
                            
                               { 
                               A 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      . However, it is not necessary to impose such an assumption (e.g., see  Remark 4.5 ), and the generality of the results in this section is illustrated by examples of curricula that do not include admissions courses (see  Example 4.4 ).

Suppose that  
                         
                            ( 
                            P 
                            , 
                            ≤ 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a partially ordered set, and let  
                         
                            p 
                            , 
                            q 
                            ∈ 
                            P 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            p 
                            ≤ 
                            q 
                         
                      . Then  
                         q 
                       
                      covers  
                      
                         p 
                       if  
                         
                            p 
                            ≠ 
                            q 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            
                               { 
                            
                            r 
                            ∈ 
                            P 
                         
                      
                      
                         | 
                      
                      
                         
                            p 
                            ≤ 
                            r 
                            ≤ 
                            q 
                            
                               } 
                            
                            = 
                            
                               { 
                               p 
                               , 
                               q 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      . It will be convenient to write  
                         
                            p 
                            ⋖ 
                            q 
                         
                       when  
                         q 
                       covers  
                         p 
                      . Also, a  chain  in  
                         P 
                       is any linearly ordered (under  
                         ≤ 
                      ) subset of  
                         P 
                      , and a chain  
                         L 
                       in  
                         P 
                       is called  saturated  if it cannot be properly extended to a larger chain by adding an element of  
                         P 
                       that lies between two elements of  
                         L 
                      ; i.e., for all  
                         
                            x 
                            , 
                            y 
                            ∈ 
                            L 
                         
                       with  
                         
                            x 
                            ≤ 
                            y 
                         
                      , if  
                         
                            p 
                            ∈ 
                            P 
                            ∖ 
                            L 
                         
                       such that  
                         
                            x 
                            ≤ 
                            p 
                            ≤ 
                            y 
                         
                       then there exists  
                         
                            q 
                            ∈ 
                            L 
                         
                       such that  
                         p 
                       and  
                         q 
                       are incomparable.

Let  
                            C 
                          be an adequate curriculum. To begin developing structure in  
                            C 
                         , a digraph is introduced that records the fulfillment of a particular rationale for declaring the candidacy of one course for being a “prerequisite” of another course. The idea is based on the perspective that, given  
                            
                               b 
                               , 
                               c 
                               ∈ 
                               C 
                            
                         , if  
                            
                               x 
                               ∈ 
                               b 
                            
                          is a task that is covered (under  
                            
                               
                                  ≲ 
                               
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                                  C 
                               
                            
                         ) by some  
                            
                               y 
                               ∈ 
                               c 
                            
                          (suggesting that there are no tasks that have to be addressed after  
                            x 
                          and before  
                            y 
                         ), then it may be appropriate to require a student to enroll in  
                            b 
                          “just before” enrolling in  
                            c 
                         . However, if  
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                          is infinite, then it could happen that no elements of  
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                          are covered by  
                            y 
                         . To resolve this scenario, the condition  
                            
                               x 
                               ⋖ 
                               y 
                            
                          will be replaced by the existence of a saturated chain  
                            L 
                          leading from  
                            x 
                          to  
                            y 
                         , and an arc  
                            
                               b 
                               → 
                               c 
                            
                          in the digraph will indicate the existence of a subchain in  
                            L 
                          of tasks in  
                            b 
                          that are not addressed in  
                            c 
                         , but lead “directly” to a subchain in  
                            L 
                          of tasks in  
                            c 
                          that lead to  
                            y 
                         . The following definition expresses this concept more precisely.

Definition 4.1 
                            If  
                                  C 
                                is an adequate curriculum then let  
                                  
                                     Γ 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        C 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                be the (simple) digraph whose vertices are the elements of  
                                  C 
                                such that  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     → 
                                     c 
                                  
                                if and only if there exists a saturated chain  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     ⋃ 
                                     C 
                                  
                                containing  
                                  
                                     ∅ 
                                     ≠ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        b 
                                        ∖ 
                                        c 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ∩ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     ∅ 
                                     ≠ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     c 
                                     ∩ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                such that  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≲ 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⋃ 
                                           C 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                for every  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                and, for every  
                                  
                                     u 
                                     ∈ 
                                     L 
                                  
                               , either  
                                  
                                     u 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≲ 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⋃ 
                                           C 
                                        
                                     
                                     x 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           1 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                or  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     
                                        
                                           ≲ 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⋃ 
                                           C 
                                        
                                     
                                     u 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           L 
                                        
                                        
                                           2 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               .

If  
                                  C 
                                is an adequate curriculum then let  
                                  
                                     Γ 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        C 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                be the (simple) digraph whose vertices are the elements of  
                                  C 
                                such that

                                     x

                                     x 
                                     ∈

The digraph  
                            
                               Γ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  C 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          will be called the  contributor digraph  of  
                            C 
                         . The condition “ 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     L 
                                  
                                  
                                     1 
                                  
                               
                               ⊆ 
                               b 
                               ∖ 
                               c 
                            
                         ” guarantees that  
                            
                               Γ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  C 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          is simple, and seems appropriate since a learner need not enroll in the course  
                            b 
                          to encounter a task  
                            x 
                          for the purpose of preparing for  
                            
                               y 
                               ∈ 
                               c 
                            
                          if  
                            
                               x 
                               ∈ 
                               c 
                            
                         . Also, the following proposition shows that  Definition 4.1  is consistent with the perspective mentioned in the above discussion, and it aids in the construction of the contributor digraphs associated with the examples of this section.

Proposition 4.2 
                            
                               Let  
                               
                                  C 
                                
                               be an adequate curriculum. If  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ⋖ 
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               for some  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     b 
                                     ∖ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                
                               then  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                     → 
                                     c 
                                  
                               
                               , and the converse holds if  
                               
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     C 
                                  
                                
                               is finite.

Let  
                               
                                  C 
                                
                               be an adequate curriculum. If  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ⋖ 
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               for some  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     b 
                                     ∖ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                
                               then  
                               
                                  
                                     b 
                                     → 
                                     c 
                                  
                               
                               , and the converse holds if  
                               
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     C 
                                  
                                
                               is finite.

Proof 
                            If  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ⋖ 
                                     y 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     b 
                                     ∖ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                then the relation  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     → 
                                     c 
                                  
                                is easily verified by setting  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        x 
                                        , 
                                        y 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               . If  
                                  C 
                                is finite then the converse follows by setting  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     = 
                                     max 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              L 
                                           
                                           
                                              1 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     = 
                                     min 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           
                                              L 
                                           
                                           
                                              2 
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                (so that there exists no element  
                                  
                                     u 
                                     ∈ 
                                     L 
                                  
                                that lies properly between  
                                  x 
                                and  
                                  y 
                               ), and then noting that  
                                  L 
                                is saturated.  □

If  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ⋖ 
                                     y 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∈ 
                                     b 
                                     ∖ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     c

                                is easily verified by setting  
                                  
                                     L 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                        x 
                                        , 
                                        y

                               . If  
                                  C 
                                is finite then the converse follows by setting  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     = 
                                     max

                                              L 
                                           
                                           
                                              1

                                     =

                                  y

The curricula given in the following two examples (which are realizable as adequate curricula by  Theorem 3.6 ) will be used to illustrate the results of this section. In particular, it will be argued that the curriculum of  Example 4.4  can be endowed with a “good” arrangement of prerequisite courses (to be made precise in Section  4.2 ), while the curriculum of  Example 4.3  cannot (see  Example 4.9 ).

                                     C 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        { 
                                     
                                     
                                        { 
                                        u 
                                        , 
                                        x 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                               ,  
                                  
                                     { 
                                     v 
                                     } 
                                  
                               ,

                                     w 
                                     , 
                                     y

                                        { 
                                        u

                                        w 
                                        ,

                                        }

                                     ⋖

                                     ⋖ 
                                     z

                                     Γ 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        C 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                is depicted in  Fig. 3 .

A  prerequisite-structure  on  
                            C 
                          is any partial order  
                            ⪯ 
                          on  
                            C 
                         , and if  
                            
                               b 
                               , 
                               c 
                               ∈ 
                               C 
                            
                          then  
                            b 
                          will be called a  prerequisite  of  
                            c 
                          if  
                            b 
                          is covered by  
                            c 
                          with respect to  
                            ⪯ 
                         . To facilitate a learner’s completion of a curriculum  
                            C 
                         , the curriculum should be endowed with a prerequisite-structure that is compatible with how the elements of  
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                          are distributed among the courses in  
                            C 
                         . For example, if it is impossible to complete a task  
                            
                               y 
                               ∈ 
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                          prior to the completion of some task  
                            
                               x 
                               ∈ 
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                         , then the prerequisite-structure on  
                            C 
                          should not admit a course containing  
                            y 
                          that is not preceded by any courses containing  
                            x 
                         . On the other hand, a well-designed prerequisite-structure will adhere to a sound rationale, and it should not impose superfluous prerequisites (although, see  Remark 4.5 ). In graph-theoretic terms, the Hasse diagram of the partially ordered set  
                            
                               ( 
                               C 
                               , 
                               ⪯ 
                               ) 
                            
                          should be a sub-digraph of  
                            
                               Γ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  C 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                         . Therefore, we seek to build curricula which satisfy the following condition.

Condition 2 
                            A prerequisite-structure  
                                  ⪯ 
                                on an adequate curriculum  
                                  C 
                                will be called  compatible  if the following two conditions are satisfied. 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (1) 
                                     For every  
                                           
                                              c 
                                              ∈ 
                                              C 
                                           
                                        , if  
                                           
                                              y 
                                              ∈ 
                                              c 
                                           
                                         and  
                                           
                                              x 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    ≲ 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ⋃ 
                                                    C 
                                                 
                                              
                                              y 
                                           
                                         for some  
                                           
                                              x 
                                              ∈ 
                                              ⋃ 
                                              C 
                                           
                                         then  
                                           
                                              b 
                                              ⪯ 
                                              c 
                                           
                                         for some  
                                           
                                              b 
                                              ∈ 
                                              C 
                                           
                                         with  
                                           
                                              x 
                                              ∈ 
                                              b 
                                           
                                         (i.e.,  
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    y

A prerequisite-structure  
                                  ⪯

(1)

For example, suppose that  
                            
                               C 
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     w 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     z 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          is an adequate curriculum, where  
                            
                               
                                  ≲ 
                               
                               
                                  ⋃ 
                                  C 
                               
                            
                          is generated by  
                            
                               w 
                               ⋖ 
                               x 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               y 
                               ⋖ 
                               z 
                            
                         . The contributor digraph  
                            
                               Γ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  C 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          consists of the three arcs  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               → 
                               
                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         ,  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               → 
                               
                                  { 
                                  z 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         , and  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               → 
                               
                                  { 
                                  z 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         
 ( Proposition 4.2 ). Hence, the linear order on  
                            C 
                          given by  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               ⪯ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               ⪯ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  z 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          satisfies  Condition 2 (2). Also, it is easy to check that  
                            ⪯ 
                          satisfies  Condition 2 (1) by noting that  
                            
                               { 
                               w 
                               , 
                               y 
                               } 
                            
                         ,  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               ∪ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         , and  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               ∪ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                               ∪ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  z 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          are semi-ideals of  
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                         . Therefore, the prerequisite-structure  
                            ⪯ 
                          is compatible (other examples of compatible prerequisite-structures are provided in  Example 4.9 (2) and  Remark 4.10 (1)).

Remark 4.5 
                            Assertion (1) of  Condition 2  ensures that no learner will encounter a task  
                                  y 
                                prior to tasks that must (in the absence of sufficiency to complete tasks from outside of the curriculum) be addressed in order to achieve sufficiency to complete  
                                  y 
                               . While this requirement is essential to student-success, assertion  (2)  just prevents  
                                  ⪯ 
                                from containing superfluous relations, and it is important to note that additional relations may be introduced into an otherwise compatible prerequisite-structure as needed. For example, if an admissions course  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ∈ 
                                     C 
                                  
                                is to be regarded in the real-world sense (that is, if  
                                  A 
                                is regarded as a collection of tasks that students have already achieved sufficiency to complete upon admittance into a program), then it should certainly happen that  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ⪯ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                for every  
                                  
                                     c 
                                     ∈ 
                                     C 
                                  
                               . Less trivially, in cases where data indicate that learners struggle to complete a task  
                                  y 
                                contained in a course  
                                  
                                     c 
                                     ∈ 
                                     C 
                                  
                               , it may be prudent to introduce a relation  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ⪯ 
                                     c 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     C 
                                  
                                containing a task  
                                  x 
                                such that  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     ∼ 
                                     y 
                                  
                               , even if  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ↛ 
                                     c 
                                  
                               . In summary, a compatible prerequisite-structure should be regarded as an essential skeleton from which additional prerequisites can be judiciously imposed.

Assertion (1) of  Condition 2  ensures that no learner will encounter a task  
                                  y 
                                prior to tasks that must (in the absence of sufficiency to complete tasks from outside of the curriculum) be addressed in order to achieve sufficiency to complete  
                                  y 
                               . While this requirement is essential to student-success, assertion  (2)  just prevents  
                                  ⪯ 
                                from containing superfluous relations, and it is important to note that additional relations may be introduced into an otherwise compatible prerequisite-structure as needed. For example, if an admissions course  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ∈ 
                                     C

                                  A 
                                is regarded as a collection of tasks that students have already achieved sufficiency to complete upon admittance into a program), then it should certainly happen that  
                                  
                                     A 
                                     ⪯ 
                                     c

                                  y

                                     b 
                                     ↛ 
                                     c

The realizability of a compatible prerequisite-structure on a curriculum depends on the courses of the curriculum. To give a trivial example of a curriculum on which there exists no compatible prerequisite-structure, let  
                            
                               C 
                               = 
                               
                                  { 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     u 
                                     , 
                                     w 
                                     }

                                     { 
                                     v 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          be the curriculum on the tasks  
                            u 
                         ,  
                            v 
                         , and  
                            w 
                          from the curriculum space given in  Example 3.3 . It was observed that  
                            C 
                          is adequate, and  
                            
                               u 
                               ⋖ 
                               v 
                               ⋖ 
                               w 
                            
                          (see the last paragraph of  Example 3.3 ). Of course,  Condition 2 (2) is trivially satisfied by any prerequisite-structure on  
                            C 
                          since  
                            
                               Γ 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  C 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          is the complete digraph given by  
                            
                               
                                  { 
                                  u 
                                  , 
                                  w 
                                  } 
                               
                               ⇆ 
                               
                                  { 
                                  v 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         
 ( Proposition 4.2 ). However,  
                            C 
                          does not admit a compatible prerequisite-structure  
                            ⪯ 
                          because  Condition 2 (1) together with the relation  
                            
                               u

                                  {

                                  , 
                                  w

                                  {

                         , while the relation

                                  { 
                                  x 
                                  , 
                                  y 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         , while the relation  
                            
                               y 
                               
                                  
                                     ≲ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ⋃ 
                                     C 
                                  
                               
                               z 
                            
                          would imply

                                  x

                                  y 
                                  }

                                  { 
                                  w 
                                  , 
                                  z 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          (a less trivial example is given in  Example 4.9 (1)). The remainder of this section is devoted to determining compatible prerequisite-structures (and whether they exist) on adequate curricula.

For advising purposes, administrators of programs are often encouraged to provide “curriculum maps” that indicate sequences of semesters of courses to be taken that lead toward completion of the program. If  
                            
                               
                                  S 
                               
                               
                                  1 
                               
                            
                          represents a semester of courses in which a student is to enroll, then it would be expected that a semester  
                            
                               
                                  S 
                               
                               
                                  2 
                               
                            
                          of courses in which the student subsequently enrolls would not contain prerequisites for courses contained in  
                            
                               
                                  S 
                               
                               
                                  1 
                               
                            
                         . Moreover, the courses of an academic curriculum are often classified by “level” (e.g., introductory, intermediate, advanced, etc.), and it is typical for a course  
                            b 
                          that is designated as a prerequisite for a course  
                            c 
                          to be categorized into a lower level than that into which  
                            c 
                          is categorized.

Let  
                            C 
                          be an adequate curriculum, and suppose that the semi-ideals of  
                            
                               ⋃ 
                               C 
                            
                          are known. The following analysis provides guidelines for building compatible prerequisite-structures by first determining the “levels” that courses should be categorized into. This is accomplished in  Algorithm 1 , which provides a greedy algorithm from which “curriculum maps” can be constructed. The desired prerequisite-structures will be defined in terms of these levels (see  Theorem 4.7 ).

The proof of  Theorem 4.7  is somewhat technical, and is postponed until the end of this section. Thus, we proceed to characterize curricula that admit compatible prerequisite-structures.

Theorem 4.8

                                  Algorithm  1 
                               .

The next example contains applications of  Theorems 4.7 and 4.8  to the curricula of  Examples 4.3 and 4.4 .

Example 4.9 
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (1) 
                                     Let  
                                           C 
                                         be the curriculum given in  Example 4.3 . Then  
                                           
                                              C 
                                              ≠ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    1 
                                                 
                                              
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    1 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ∪ 
                                              ⋯ 
                                              ∪ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    N 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                         (see  Example 4.6 (1)), so  
                                           C 
                                         does not admit a compatible prerequisite-structure by  Theorem 4.8 . 
                                  
                                  
                                     (2) 
                                     Let  
                                           C 
                                         be the curriculum given in  Example 4.4 . Then  
                                           
                                              C 
                                              = 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    1 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ∪ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    2 
                                                 
                                              
                                              ∪ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    3 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                         (see  Example 4.6 (2)), so  
                                           C 
                                         admits a compatible prerequisite-structure by  Theorem 4.8 . In fact, the partial order  
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⪯ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 C 
                                              
                                           
                                         on  
                                           C 
                                         (whose Hasse diagram is provided in  Fig. 4 ) is compatible by  Theorem 4.7 .

(1) 
                                     Let  
                                           C 
                                         be the curriculum given in  Example 4.3 . Then

                                              ≠

                                              ⋯ 
                                              ∪ 
                                              
                                                 
                                                    S 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    N 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                         (see  Example 4.6 (1)), so  
                                           C 
                                         does not admit a compatible prerequisite-structure by  Theorem 4.8 .

                                           C 
                                         be the curriculum given in  Example 4.4 . Then

                                         (see  Example 4.6 (2)), so  
                                           C 
                                         admits a compatible prerequisite-structure by  Theorem 4.8 . In fact, the partial order  
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⪯ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 C 
                                              
                                           
                                         on  
                                           C

                                         (see  Example 4.6 (1)), so  
                                           C 
                                         does not admit a compatible prerequisite-structure by  Theorem 4.8 .

                                              =

                                                    3 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                         (see  Example 4.6 (2)), so  
                                           C

Remark 4.10 
                            
                               
                            
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (1) 
                                     The above results show that an adequate curriculum  
                                           C 
                                         admits a compatible prerequisite-structure if and only if the partial order  
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⪯ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 C 
                                              
                                           
                                         is a compatible prerequisite-structure on  
                                           C 
                                        . However, note that compatible prerequisite-structures need not be unique. For example, it is easy to check that the prerequisite-structure  
                                           ⪯ 
                                         on the curriculum  
                                           C 
                                         given in  Example 4.4  that is generated by  
                                           
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 x 
                                                 , 
                                                 y 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ⪯ 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 u 
                                                 , 
                                                 z 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ⪯ 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 v 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ⪯ 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 w 
                                                 , 
                                                 y 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                           
                                         (and  
                                           
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 u 
                                                 , 
                                                 x 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                              ⪯ 
                                              
                                                 { 
                                                 u 
                                                 , 
                                                 x 
                                                 } 
                                              
                                           
                                        ) is compatible. 
                                  
                                  
                                     (2) 
                                     Even if a curriculum admits an adequate prerequisite-structure, it may not be the most efficient curriculum (e.g., regarding  Example 4.4 , the prerequisite-structure  
                                           ⪯ 
                                         defined in  (1)  above may be preferred over  
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⪯ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 C 
                                              
                                           
                                         since, under the prerequisite-structure  
                                           ⪯ 
                                        , the course  
                                           
                                              { 
                                              u 
                                              , 
                                              x 
                                              } 
                                           
                                         can be eliminated from the curriculum without forfeiting any tasks). On the other hand, sometimes there exist pedagogical benefits to requiring learners to encounter tasks multiple times in a curriculum (e.g., the context in which a given topic is presented often varies from course to course), and it may be counterproductive to whittle curricula into fragmented collections of tasks. Nevertheless, it seems valuable to determine whether  
                                           C 
                                         contains a proper sub-curriculum that admits a compatible prerequisite-structure while covering every task that is covered in  
                                           C 
                                        , particularly in cases where  
                                           C 
                                         admits no compatible prerequisite-structure. The next corollary provides complete answers to such inquiries.

(1) 
                                     The above results show that an adequate curriculum  
                                           C 
                                         admits a compatible prerequisite-structure if and only if the partial order  
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⪯ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 C 
                                              
                                           
                                         is a compatible prerequisite-structure on  
                                           C 
                                        . However, note that compatible prerequisite-structures need not be unique. For example, it is easy to check that the prerequisite-structure  
                                           ⪯ 
                                         on the curriculum  
                                           C 
                                         given in  Example 4.4  that is generated by
